title: fagara mantchurica f. grandifolia
intro: Fagara mantchurica f. grandifolia, also known as the Large-leaved Chinese Pepper, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains a high concentration of oils and alkaloids, particularly caryophyllene and borneol, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Fagara mantchurica f. grandifolia include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Fagara mantchurica f. grandifolia is often prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties and is typically used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
